mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market, which translates to higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market health is a primary driver of consumer spending, which constitutes a majority of GDP.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between PAYEMS 3-month change and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is less than 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a three-month period signals a sudden deterioration in the labor market, leading to expectations of lower consumer spending and corporate profits, causing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer spending (RSAFS) growth is decelerating, a concurrent rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) disproportionately harms equity valuations by tightening financial conditions when the consumer is already weak, leading to negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Classic monetary policy transmission channel impacting a weakening economy.
failure_condition: An interaction term between RSAFS momentum and FEDFUNDS changes is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a predictive model over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment with deflationary fears, a modest acceleration in inflation (CPIAUCSL) is interpreted positively by markets as a sign of returning pricing power and aggregate demand, boosting short-term returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC market narrative was dominated by concerns about deflation, not inflation.
failure_condition: An increase in the 3-month change of CPIAUCSL is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time during the 2009-2012 period.

mechanism_narrative: A negative trend in industrial production (INDPRO) growth reflects weakening manufacturing and business investment, directly implying lower future earnings for a significant portion of the S&P 500 and thus leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key component of the Conference Board Leading Economic Index.
failure_condition: The correlation between the INDPRO 6-month change and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) below a key activity level signals a severe loss of confidence in a critical economic sector, leading to broad negative expectations and lower equity prices over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:The 2007-2009 housing crisis demonstrated the sector's systemic importance.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 800k, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly lower than periods when it is above 800k (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment (UNRATE) and slowing real GDP growth (GDPC1) is a definitive recessionary signal. This joint condition indicates widespread economic distress, leading to sharp declines in expected corporate earnings and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 6-month change > 0.2 AND GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:NBER recession dating criteria involve coincident weakness in employment and output.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 during triggered periods fails to produce a statistically significant negative alpha over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is above a restrictive threshold, it increases the discount rate for future earnings and makes lower-risk assets more attractive, creating downward pressure on equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:High rates preceded the 2000 and 2007 market peaks.
failure_condition: During periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is positive and greater than the long-term average unconditional return.
